element_introduction_order.mastery_context: establishing hostile environment with Skynet machines
urban defensive scenarios with increased enemy density
complex enemy encounters in confined spaces
vehicle-based action sequences alternating with platforming

typical_relief_triggers: driving level sequences break platforming tension
checkpoint placements (though noted as spaced too far apart)
typical_tension_triggers: confined space sequences in police station and nightclub
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: uneven pacing with difficulty spikes; tension sustained through enemy encounters punctuated by driving sequences

difficulty_curve.early_game: introduces Kyle Reese control in future war setting with basic platforming
difficulty_curve.mid_game: escalates to police station and nightclub scenarios with increased enemy density and confined spaces
difficulty_curve.late_game: culminates with complex enemy encounters and sustained Terminator threat presence
notable_spikes: checkpoints placed too far apart causing frustration on difficult sections
exceedingly difficult platforming sequences noted by reviewers
confined space combat in nightclub level

notable_level_patterns.description: 2029 Skynet war setting establishing baseline threat; features platforming through hostile machine-infested environments
Los Angeles 1984 locations including police station and nightclub; transitions from outdoor to confined indoor spaces with escalating enemy encounters
Vehicle-based action sequences interspersed throughout campaign to provide pacing relief from platforming; praised by reviewers for variety

sources_used: wikipedia - List of Terminator video games
